mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding economic output, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rising INDPRO periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5%, labor market slack signals a severe economic downturn, causing investors to price in falling corporate revenues and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in consumer price inflation and the federal funds rate indicate a tightening monetary policy response to overheating, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields negative Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns do not decline when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, elevated housing starts and retail sales reflect peak consumer demand that is unsustainable, leading to an imminent demand pullback and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or flat during high HOUST and RSAFS periods in late-cycle regimes, failing to predict the anticipated demand contraction.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects strengthening labor demand, which boosts consumer spending expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS momentum is consistently below 0.02 over 1-year rolling windows, or 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 320.0, elevated inflation erodes real corporate margins and triggers expectations of aggressive central bank intervention, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL crosses above 320.0, or the threshold shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising real GDP growth and declining unemployment signals a synchronized economic recovery, which improves earnings outlooks and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction term yields negative or null predictive accuracy over 3-year rolling windows, or forward returns do not increase when GDP rises alongside falling unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery regime, any positive momentum in industrial production indicates rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during post-shock recovery periods show no positive correlation with INDPRO momentum, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.
